pages.raw_text:
The Cement Workers Union No.
1873 has petitioned the city council
to pass an ordinance fixing the
wages of cement workers at $500
per day. The union asks for this
increase of so cents per day as the
cement workers cannot secure
work more than six months in the
year and they claim it is a diff
cult matter to make both ends meet
on their present scale OF wages.


The list of unions affiliated with
the Central Labor union continues
to grow. Last night carpenters
union No. 1860 sent in credentials
which were accepted AMI the dele,
gates seated. The plumbers local
No. A, wishing to ad in making
Spokane the best union town in the
west, also sent credentials which
were accepted and their delegates
seated.


The local baker and confection-
ers presented their scale of wages
to the Central union last night for
the endorsement of that body,
which was given. The new scale
calls for 81 per week more money
and has been accepted by the local
manufacturers. The new scale is
operative for one year.


A communication has been re-
ceived from President Gompers of
the American Federation of Labor
informing the Central Labor union
that he had commissioned E. A
Cooney local organizer for the Fed
Grafton. Mr.. Cooney's appointment
meets with favor among local
union men.


Application was made some time
ago by the local cement workers
union for a charter from the Inter
